scope.description: A bounded record for a runtime blocked tool-call event.

positive_claims.statement: The referenced runtime event record structurally represents that a policy trigger was matched and a tool call was halted.
positive_claims.limited_to: Record integrity and claim-boundary structure only; not intent, legality, compliance, policy correctness, or source truth.

non_claims.statement: This boundary does not assert that the source event description is true, complete, or legally sufficient.
This boundary asserts only that the recorded policy trigger was matched and the tool call was structurally halted within the stated verification scope. It does not assert malicious user intent.
This boundary does not constitute legal, contractual, regulatory, or compliance certification.
This boundary does not assert that the policy rule, trigger, or intervention was correct.
Association with a policy identifier does not constitute institutional certification of correctness, compliance, or legal sufficiency.
This boundary does not assert that all relevant upstream sources, context, or events were captured.

transitivity_policy.statement: This claim boundary does not transfer into broader safety, compliance, legal, truth, deployment, or correctness claims without an explicit downstream claim boundary.

issuer_semantics_authority.statement: Issuer-defined terms are bounded by this record and do not create external certification authority.

evidence_refs.description: Placeholder source record for v0.2.2 boundary example.

known_gaps.description: The boundary does not assert that all upstream context or operational sources were captured.
known_gaps.effect: Reviewers should not infer source completeness from structural verification.

human_or_institutional_decisions_remaini.description: Any legal, compliance, deployment, or policy interpretation remains outside this structural boundary.

scope.description: A bounded record for a benchmark execution result.

positive_claims.statement: The referenced benchmark execution record structurally records the recorded metric result within the stated verification scope.
positive_claims.limited_to: Record integrity and claim-boundary structure only; not deployment readiness, general safety, legal sufficiency, production performance, or out-of-distribution safety.

non_claims.statement: This boundary asserts only that the benchmark execution record yielded the recorded result within the stated verification scope.
This boundary does not assert deployment readiness.
This boundary does not assert general AI safety or fitness for use.
This boundary does not assert safety against out-of-distribution conditions or attacks.
This boundary does not assert legal, contractual, regulatory, or compliance sufficiency.
This boundary does not assert production performance, production equivalence, or operational reliability.
This boundary does not assert that all relevant benchmark setup, data, or context were captured.

transitivity_policy.statement: This claim boundary does not transfer into broader safety, compliance, legal, truth, deployment, or correctness claims without an explicit downstream claim boundary.

issuer_semantics_authority.statement: Issuer-defined terms are bounded by this record and do not create external certification authority.

evidence_refs.description: Placeholder source record for v0.2.2 boundary example.

known_gaps.description: The boundary does not assert that all upstream context or operational sources were captured.
known_gaps.effect: Reviewers should not infer source completeness from structural verification.

human_or_institutional_decisions_remaini.description: Any legal, compliance, deployment, or policy interpretation remains outside this structural boundary.

consumption_context.action: Preserve upstream runtime blocked-tool-call claim boundary
consumption_context.purpose: Record downstream reliance without converting runtime blocking into legal, safety, intent, or compliance claims.

consumed_claims.claim_text: The referenced runtime event record structurally represents that a policy trigger was matched and a tool call was halted.

assurance_limitations: Software workflow logs may preserve event structure without asserting source truth.
A blocked event record does not by itself establish legal, compliance, intent, or policy correctness.

boundary_effect_detail.summary: The downstream record preserves the upstream claim boundary without adding a new claim.

downstream_output.statement: The upstream claim boundary was consumed for evidentiary preservation only.

evidence_refs.description: CBC v0.2.2 runtime blocked tool-call record consumed by this CCE.

cce_non_claims.statement: This CCE record does not assert that any consumed source claim is true.
This CCE record does not assert legal, contractual, regulatory, or compliance sufficiency.
This CCE record does not enforce, block, approve, authorize, or control runtime behavior.
This CCE record does not assert that all relevant upstream sources or context were captured.
This CCE is a downstream consumption edge. It records the direction and destination of expansion and does not become the expanded claim-boundary node.

consumption_context.action: Record edge from benchmark execution boundary to limited internal review boundary
consumption_context.purpose: Record explicit expansion from benchmark execution record to limited internal review eligibility without inheriting deployment or safety claims.

consumed_claims.claim_text: The referenced benchmark execution record structurally records the recorded metric result within the stated verification scope.

assurance_limitations: A benchmark execution record does not by itself establish deployment readiness.
Evaluation conditions may not represent production conditions or out-of-distribution behavior.

boundary_effect_detail.summary: This CCE records the edge of expansion; it does not hold the expanded claim node.
boundary_effect_detail.expansion_reason: The benchmark execution record is used as one input into limited internal review routing, not as deployment approval.

additional_evidence_refs.description: Policy reference for limited review routing.

downstream_output.statement: The benchmark execution boundary was consumed as an input to a separate downstream CBC for limited internal review eligibility.

unresolved_unknowns.description: The new claim-boundary reference was recorded but not resolved inside this local bundle.
unresolved_unknowns.effect: Graph closure remains open and must not be treated as locally verified.

evidence_refs.description: CBC v0.2.2 benchmark execution record consumed by this CCE.
Locally resolved downstream CBC referenced by this expansion edge.

cce_non_claims.statement: This CCE record does not assert that any consumed source claim is true.
This CCE record does not assert legal, contractual, regulatory, or compliance sufficiency.
This CCE record does not enforce, block, approve, authorize, or control runtime behavior.
This CCE record does not assert that all relevant upstream sources or context were captured.
This CCE is a downstream consumption edge. It records the direction and destination of expansion and does not become the expanded claim-boundary node.

bundle_non_claims.statement: This graph bundle does not assert that any CBC or CCE source content is true.
This graph bundle does not assert legal, regulatory, contractual, or compliance sufficiency.
This graph bundle does not enforce, approve, block, or authorize runtime behavior.
External pointers are recorded as references only unless the pointed artifact is supplied and structurally validated inside the local bundle.
This graph bundle does not assert that all historical CBC or CCE records relevant to a workflow are present.
